confirmed.note: Dual-write 'sync liveness flags to SessionStore' block copy-pasted: verbatim comment + SessionStore::Get().Transform(hashKeyID,[&](const CanonicalSession& cs){...}) lambda, body partially edited (block 1 sets all 3 flags from entry; block 2 conditionally sets one flag by lane)

Secondary-index lookups LookupBySessionId/ByAddress/ByGenesis/ByIP are 4 cloned bodies; only the mapXToKey index name and param type/name differ: auto optKey=mapXToKey.Get(x); if(!optKey) return nullopt; return mapSessions.Get(optKey.value()); reducible to one template helper

false_positives.what_happened: Concurrency-test scaffolding: every Catch2 SECTION wraps its work in the identical frame for(int t=0;t<nThreads;++t){threads.emplace_back([&]{ for(...) {BODY} });} for(auto& thread:threads) thread.join(); REQUIRE(...). Token detector latches onto the spawn/emplace_back/join boilerplate; only the lambda BODY (the unit under test) actually differs, so the matched span is the test harness, not real logic duplication
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add a test-scaffold filter — when a matched span's high-similarity lines are dominated by thread/loop-control + framework macros (threads.emplace_back, thread.join(), TEST_CASE/SECTION/REQUIRE) and the interior lambda/loop body diverges, down-weight or drop. Concretely: require the matched chunk to contain >=N consecutive non-control, non-macro statement lines before counting, and exclude lines matching emplace_back/\.join\(\)/REQUIRE/SECTION from the similarity numerator

false_positives.what_happened: API-command template boilerplate: every TAO::API Mining::Xxx endpoint file shares the identical Nexus license banner, the same include block, and the same skeleton namespace TAO::API { encoding::json Mining::Xxx(const encoding::json& jParams, const bool fHelp){ if(fHelp) return std::string("...help..."); ... }. Cross-file detector matches banner+includes+help-prologue that the project mandates for every command, not copied business logic
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: strip the shared license/comment banner before hashing (already partially done), and add a per-project 'command-template prologue' suppressor — detect that the matched cross-file span is bounded to the leading include cluster + a function signature + a fHelp early-return string literal, and exclude it. Generally: exclude included-header lines and string-literal-only lines (help text) from cross-file similarity scoring so only executable statements count

intro_commits.edge: wallettx.h adds #include <Legacy/wallet/wallet.h> AND wallet.h adds #include <Legacy/types/wallettx.h> (both in same commit)
intro_commits.reason: Header-split refactor ('Break up some headers into their proper source files'): wallettx.h was newly extracted from /dev/null and both headers were wired to include each other, creating a back-reference cycle. NOTE: this is a 2019 commit by VidereLicet — original Nexus/LLL-TAO code, predates the 2025-05+ AI-authoring window; the cycle is inherited, not AI-introduced drift

cycle.evidence: Authored code in src/Legacy/ (wallet subsystem), not vendor. The only other mutual include pair, src/LLD/hash/xxhash.c <-> xxhash.h, is the vendored xxHash amalgamation (Yann Collet, BSD-2; xxhash.h line 433 #include "xxhash.c" guarded by XXH_PRIVATE_API for static inlining) — a normal vendor idiom, not counted as an authored cycle
